Time to nerd schizo rant for a second.

"Hxr1" writes you can abuse the newly minted Windows Machine Learning API to execute shellcode in-memory without having to invoke VirtualAlloc.

That is wrong .
replyText:
Love the breakdown.
 So the WinML API itself isn't the vulnerability—it's just a convoluted file loader in this case. The real trick would be finding a way to get RWX without calling VirtualAlloc, right?
